title: Chainlink Expands CCIP to Eight Blockchains
description: Chainlink has announced a large-scale expansion of its Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP), adding support for eight new blockchain networks, including Avalanche, Ethereum, Polygon, and five additional chains. This development represents one of the most significant infrastructure upgrades in the decentralized finance space this quarter.
lead: Chainlink has announced the expansion of the CCIP protocol to eight new blockchains
author: Editorial

children.value: Chainlink has announced a large-scale expansion of its

, adding support for eight new blockchain networks, including Avalanche, Ethereum, Polygon, and five additional chains. This development represents one of the most significant infrastructure upgrades in the decentralized finance space this quarter.
The expansion allows developers to build applications that can interact seamlessly across different blockchain ecosystems, addressing one of DeFi's most persistent problems —
According to DefiLlama, cross-chain protocols currently manage more than
in total value locked, while demand for interoperability solutions has grown 340% year over year.

The protocol uses Chainlink's decentralized oracle network to verify cross-chain transactions, implementing a
that monitors unusual activity across all supported networks.
Early testing showed a transaction success rate above 99.8% with an average settlement time of under 10 minutes.
, have already begun integrating CCIP functionality. Aave reported a 25% increase in cross-chain lending activity during beta testing.

For decentralized autonomous organizations, the expansion enables more sophisticated treasury management across multiple networks. DAOs can now execute governance decisions spanning different blockchain ecosystems without the need for multiple separate proposals.

The integration with Layer 2 solutions is particularly significant given Ethereum's current scaling challenges. With L2 solutions processing more than 4 million transactions daily, the CCIP expansion could enable more efficient asset movement between scaling solutions, potentially reducing user costs by

The rollout will proceed in stages over the next six weeks. Integrations with Avalanche and Polygon are scheduled first, with the remaining networks to follow. Early adopters report significant improvements in capital efficiency, with some protocols seeing 60% faster cross-chain settlements.
